notes: The twelve tableaux of Northwind Harbor · one held wide shot each. Adjacency is the walkable graph; sound_cue is what the location sounds like from one screen away (Chapter 6 fog navigation swaps nav labels for these). worker/ambient_lines are the town talking AT you while working.

locations.name: UNCLE MADDOX'S PORCH
locations.sound_cue: a screen door on a spring
ambient_lines: maddox, not looking up from the nets · 'back by the horns.'
maddox · 'dog knows the route better than you. let him think he's deciding.'

locations.name: THE CHURCH STEPS
locations.sound_cue: a bell rope knocking its post
ambient_lines: mrs. ansel, sweeping the steps · 'fog by thursday. my knee says.'
mrs. ansel · 'nobody rings it before seven. town rule older than the church.'

locations.name: THE BAIT SHOP
locations.sound_cue: a coffee pot going on a camp stove
ambient_lines: ruthie · 'pot's always on. that's the whole business model, kid.'
ruthie · 'herring under the lamp, sand shrimp off the flat. write it down or don't.'

locations.name: THE CHANDLERY
locations.sound_cue: a lamp ballast buzzing over the door
ambient_lines: holt, restocking shackles · 'everything on this wall fits something in this harbor. that's the inventory system.'
holt · 'the chart's older than the paint. don't lean on it.'

locations.name: THE FERRY SLIP
locations.sound_cue: a ticket window sliding open and shut
locations.worker: the young clerk (GLASS, the badge says)
ambient_lines: the clerk, GLASS on the badge, squaring a stack of forms · 'ferry's at ten past. it was at ten past yesterday. it will be at ten past tomorrow.'
glass · 'i keep everything people leave. someone always comes back. usually.'

locations.name: THE FISH-CLEANING STATION
locations.sound_cue: knives on steel and gulls fighting over it
locations.worker: Dot and Vera, cleaning the night boat's catch
ambient_lines: dot, not slowing · 'gull gets one in three. that's the arrangement. nobody signed it.'
vera · 'you can tell the year by the knife. this one's my mother's. it remembers bigger fish.'

locations.name: THE CANNERY WALL
locations.sound_cue: wind working a sheet of tin somewhere high
ambient_lines: the cannery's been shut since before you were born. the wall keeps its posters anyway.
layers of paper on the brick · auctions, boats for sale, a dance. years of it.

locations.name: THE CO-OP ICE HOUSE
ambient_lines: andersen, moving a block with tongs like it's nothing · 'cold keeps everything. that's its one idea.'
andersen · 'co-op owns the ice, the scale-weights, and one truck. everything else is borrowed.'

locations.name: THE FUEL DOCK
locations.sound_cue: the sodium lamp's buzz over the pumps
ambient_lines: karl, coiling line with raw hands · 'tide does the real work. we just stand near it and complain.'
karl · 'lamp's been buzzing that same note since '79. i'd miss it.'

locations.name: THE JETTY
ambient_lines: the planks give a little. they always have. nobody's fallen through yet.
rope coils, crab pots, a bucket someone will come back for.

locations.name: THE SEINER CLARA B.
ambient_lines: byrne, up to his elbows in the engine box · 'she's older than my marriage and runs better. usually.'
byrne · 'you hear the horns, that's us or the ferry. learn the difference and you'll never be lost.'

locations.name: THE BREAKWATER
ambient_lines: anna vail, trimming the breakwater light · 'harbor's a room. this is the door. somebody has to hold it.'
anna · 'i had a boat once. now i have the light. it's the same job from the other side.'
